title: Coleus nitidus
intro: The Coleus nitidus, also known as the shining coleus, is a plant species of the genus Coleus, characterized by its vibrant purple flowers and shiny, dark green leaves. Traditionally, the Coleus nitidus has been used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and analgesic properties. The bioactive constituents of Coleus nitidus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Coleus nitidus is often prepared as a decoction or a powder, and is commonly used to make Ayurvedic remedies such as teas, capsules, and ointments.
taxonomy: Coleus nitidus is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It is native to tropical regions and is commonly known as the 'butterfly plant'. This plant belongs to the genus Coleus, which contains numerous species of flowering plants. Coleus nitidus is often used as an ornamental plant in gardens due to its vibrant and colorful foliage. The classification of Coleus nitidus is further divided into various cultivars based on its unique leaf patterns and colors.
